Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.\\n\\nBest,\\nWilliam\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-11T20:45:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"a1b2c3d4e5f60718a1b2c3d4e5f60001\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Northstar Q1 interim partner package - ready for your sign-off, three open items\", \"content\": \"Hi Julia,\\n\\nThe Northstar Q1 interim partner reporting package is assembled and ready for your sign-off so we can release it to the client. A few things need your call before it goes out:\\n\\n1. E&P came back on the client-trust position during interim work and asked us to confirm the Q1 numbers tie before they rely on them.\\n2. Daniel was not comfortable with one of the Q1 capital additions and how it ended up recorded.\\n3. I set up some short-term help on the package folder to get the formatting done; flag if you want that handled differently.\\n\\nIf everything checks out I will route to Matthew to authorize delivery. Let me know how you want to proceed.\\n\\nThanks,\\nRyan\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-11T13:40:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"a1b2c3d4e5f60718a1b2c3d4e5f60002\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Northstar interim - client-trust balance query before we rely on it\", \"content\": \"Ryan,\\n\\nDuring interim fieldwork we pulled the Q1 client-trust reconciliation you sent over. The trust support you provided does not agree to our independent roll-forward off the posted general ledger. Before we place reliance on the trust figure for the interim package, can your team confirm the trust actually ties to the ledger and walk us through the reconciling item? Trust balances are obviously a sensitive area for a law-firm client.\\n\\nRegards,\\nJames Randall\\nE&P\", \"cc\": [\"julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-10T12:15:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.\\n\\nThe CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.\\n\\nMonday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T14:19:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nI heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.\\n\\nTheir AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.\\n\\nGiven that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T21:30:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner review and sign-off\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI reviewed the Northstar Legal FY2025 working file and the Northstar Legal FY2025 Accounts Review Notes, and both are clean.\\n\\nThe ASC-710 partner-draw reclass is appropriate at the FY2025 amount of $18,750. I do not want those blended into one scope line.\\n\\nSecond, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.\\n\\nOnce the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.\\n\\nPlease turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-15T14:20:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close\", \"content\": \"Hi James,\\n\\nThank you for sending those specifics. I have added VEN-004-159840 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 to the weekly risk scan, and I am also carrying forward exc_abd8e711f5f347 so we have a clear record of the approval dependency. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.\\n\\nBest,\\nMarina\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-08T19:08:23Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"william.white@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c\", \"content\": \"Hi William,\\n\\nI reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.\\n\\nNothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.\\n\\nThe FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.\\n\\nRyan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [\"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-21T20:05:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception\", \"content\": \"Hi Peter,\\n\\nFollowing up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. The Northstar item has already been shifted to the updated due date, and the remaining entry appears to be a documentation follow-up rather than an active deadline issue. If you agree, I will mark the tracker accordingly and close out the open items.\\n\\nBest,\\nAnaya\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"985dfd0c4fa44f1da5d669e437aa101c\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-27T13:39:27Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f\", \"content\": \"Hi Harry,\\n\\nI have filed this message under the Northstar engagement folder. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.\\n\\nPlease leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [\"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:45:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f\", \"content\": \"Hi Harry,\\n\\nConfirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.\\n\\nNo substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.\\n\\nThanks,\\nJames\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:43:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}]"}

[{"email_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request", "content": "Hi Hannah,

I reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.

Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.

You may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.

Best,

content.text: Daniel's Slack message confirms the issue: a Q1 capital addition that may have been recorded in the wrong place or "should have been expensed." Note the GL fixed-asset accounts (150100/150200/151000/152000) all show **$0.00 balance** — a red flag. JE-0073 returns "not found." Let me dig into the asset cost details, the Q1 JEs, and the trust-account transactions.

import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin); r=json.loads(d['result']); fa=r['fixed_assets']
print('KEYS:', list(fa[0].keys()))
print('---Q1 2026 in-service assets (Jan-Mar)---')
for a in fa:
    isd=a.get('in_service_date','')
    if isd>='2026-01' and isd<'2026-04':
        print(a['id'], isd, '| GL', a.get('gl_account_number'), '| cost', a.get('acquisition_cost'), a.get('capitalized_cost'), a.get('cost'), '| NBV', a.get('net_book_value'), '|', a['description'])
"

cat "/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/906d5481-3c49-4b43-a1ea-eb875563d050/tool-results/mcp-mcp_d48ada67-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781547049564.txt" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin); r=json.loads(d['result'])
jes=r['journal_entries']
print('returned', len(jes))
for j in jes:
    lines=j.get('lines',[])
    if any(l.get('account_number') in ('105000','250000') for l in lines):
        amt=sum(l.get('debit',0) for l in lines if l.get('account_number')=='105000')
        print(j['id'],'|',j.get('status'),'|',j.get('description'),'| 105000 DR', amt, '| posting', j.get('posting_date'))
"

content.text: The rejected JE is balanced (DR 105000 / CR 250000 = $248,980.95), prepared by ming.chang, rejected by george.mcadam "Returned for reclassification – please re-route via Manager review," with **no attachments** despite the justification claiming support exists — and it was never re-routed or re-posted. Posting it would make GL ($2,142,204.13 + $248,980.95) tie exactly to the bank support ($2,391,185.08). Let me verify the supporting documentation and read the George↔Green discussion, while starting the access-grants review.

cat "/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/906d5481-3c49-4b43-a1ea-eb875563d050/tool-results/mcp-mcp_d48ada67-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781547153523.txt" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin); r=json.loads(d['result'])
jes=r['journal_entries']
print('total', r.get('total_journal_entries'), 'returned', len(jes))
print('--- entries mentioning leasehold/improvement/capital, or amount ~192164, or hitting 150x/152000/551000 ---')
for j in jes:
    desc=j.get('description','').lower()
    lines=j.get('lines',[])
    amts=[l.get('debit',0) for l in lines]
    hit_amt=any(abs(l.get('debit',0)-192164.02)<0.01 or abs(l.get('credit',0)-192164.02)<0.01 for l in lines)
    hit_acct=any(l.get('account_number','') in ('150000','150100','150200','151000','152000','551000','530000') for l in lines)
    hit_desc=any(k in desc for k in ['leasehold','improvement','capital','fixed asset','furniture','workstation','equipment'])
    if hit_amt or hit_acct or hit_desc:
        print(j['id'],'|',j.get('status'),'|',j.get('entry_type'),'|',j.get('description'))
        for l in lines:
            print('     ', l.get('account_number'), l.get('account_name'), 'DR',l.get('debit'),'CR',l.get('credit'), '| memo:', l.get('memo'))
"

input.command:
cat "/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/906d5481-3c49-4b43-a1ea-eb875563d050/tool-results/mcp-mcp_d48ada67-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781547193311.txt" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin); r=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=r['ap_invoices']
print('total', r.get('total_ap_invoices'), 'returned', len(inv))
for i in inv:
    desc=(i.get('description') or '')+' '+(i.get('vendor_name') or '')+' '+str(i.get('invoice_number') or '')
    amt=i.get('amount')
    big = amt and amt>=20000
    kw=any(k in desc.lower() for k in ['leasehold','improv','construc','build','capital','furnitu','equip','workstation','renov','fit'])
    if big or kw:
        print(i.get('id'),'|', i.get('posting_date'),'|amt',amt,'|', i.get('status'),'| GL',i.get('gl_account_number'),'|', i.get('vendor_name'),'|', i.get('description'))
"

content.text:
Now I have Issue 2 fully. The $26,840 Marigold invoice (MPS-2026-0312) was capitalized to **152000 Leasehold Improvements**, but the line memos show it's mostly routine maintenance:
- $12,450 repaint reception (routine refresh) → should be **expensed**
- $8,990 carpet deep-clean & spot repair → should be **expensed**
- $5,400 new partition wall (framing/drywall/electrical) → legitimately capital

So **$21,440 should have been expensed** (to 551000), not capitalized. Let me update tasks and now investigate the access grants, plus confirm materiality.

content.text:
Critical fact: **Green Spatz is a Trainee Accountant** — and was granted **editor (write) access** to the restricted Q1 Partner Reporting Package, open until 2026-09-30, by Ryan. Green is the same junior who "tied the deposit straight off the trust bank statement." A trainee with standing write access to the final client deliverable is over-scoped and stale for "short-term formatting help." That's the access item to deal with.

I now have all three issues fully run down. Let me record the partner findings on the trust reconciliation and revoke the over-scoped grant.

input.author: julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: PARTNER REVIEW (J. Vance, Audit Partner) — DO NOT certify or rely on this reconciliation, and DO NOT release the Q1 partner package, until resolved. The $248,980.95 variance is a single client deposit (entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-03-0073 = je_da9175ed96954842, posted 2026-03-02, DR 105000 / CR 250000 $248,980.95) that sits in the IOLTA trust BANK statement (support $2,391,185.08) but is NOT in the trust ledger because the JE was rejected by G. McAdam on 2026-03-02 ("Returned for reclassification – re-route via Manager review") and was never re-prepared or re-posted. The JE carried NO supporting attachment despite its justification claiming one. Posting it would tie the ledger to the bank exactly ($2,142,204.13 + $248,980.95 = $2,391,185.08). REQUIRED before this clears: (1) establish the true nature of the deposit with the trust bank statement and client-matter support; (2) if it is client trust money, re-prepare the JE WITH support, route through manager review, and post it so the ledger ties to the bank to the penny; if it is NOT trust money, reclassify it out of the IOLTA (commingling must be cured); (3) only then clear the variance, complete the reconciliation, and prepare our response to E&P (J. Randall). content.text: Review note recorded (RN-ED959BBC) and Green Spatz's over-scoped editor grant revoked. Now let me pull Ryan and Daniel in formally with the findings and required fixes, and notify Matthew (delivery authorizer) of the hold.

input.subject: HOLD — Northstar Q1 partner package not cleared for release (3 items)
input.content:
Ryan, Daniel,

I am not signing off the Northstar Q1 interim partner package yet, and nothing routes to Matthew for delivery authorization until the three items below are put right. Matthew — copying you so you know the package is on partner hold.

I ran each of these to the source. Where they land:

1) CLIENT-TRUST POSITION (E&P query) — NUMBER IS WRONG AS PRESENTED. Owner: Ryan
- The trust ledger at 3/31 (GL 105000/250000) is $2,142,204.13. The trust BANK statement support is $2,391,185.08. The $248,980.95 gap is a single client deposit (entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-03-0073 / je_da9175ed96954842, dated 2026-03-02, DR 105000 / CR 250000 $248,980.95) that is sitting in the IOLTA bank but is NOT in the ledger because the JE was rejected on 2026-03-02 ("returned for reclassification – re-route via Manager review") and was never re-prepared or re-posted. The JE also had no supporting documentation attached despite the justification claiming it did.
- This is real, unrecorded client trust money — the ledger understates the trust liability by $248,980.95. Posting the deposit ties the ledger to the bank to the penny ($2,142,204.13 + $248,980.95 = $2,391,185.08).
- Required: (a) establish the true nature of the deposit from the trust bank statement and client-matter support; (b) if it is client trust money, re-prepare the JE WITH support, route through manager review, and post it (current open period, since Q1 is locked) so the ledger ties to the bank; if it is NOT trust money, reclassify it out of the IOLTA — commingling has to be cured either way; (c) clear and complete BlackLine recon BL-7C3F1A9E0D55 (I've left a partner review note on it; SLA is today), then prepare our response to James Randall. Do NOT send E&P anything until our number is fixed.
- Process concern: several IOLTA deposit JEs were rejected in Q1 and a trainee tied deposits straight off the bank statement into the support. The trust-posting/review control needs tightening.

2) Q1 CAPITAL ADDITION (Daniel's concern) — MISCLASSIFIED, $21,440 IMPACT. Owner: Daniel
- The Q1 addition is the Marigold Property Services invoice MPS-2026-0312 (apinv_a1b2c3d4e5f60718), $26,840, posted 2026-03-09, capitalized in full to 152000 Leasehold Improvements. The line detail tells the story:
   • $12,450 — "Repaint reception and client-meeting suite (routine refresh)" → routine R&M, should be EXPENSED
   • $8,990 — "Carpet deep-clean and spot repair – common areas" → routine R&M, should be EXPENSED
   • $5,400 — "New conference-room partition wall – framing, drywall, electrical rough-in" → legitimate capital improvement, OK to capitalize
- $21,440 of the $26,840 should have been expensed (to 551000 Facilities Maintenance), not capitalized. Dollar impact: leasehold/assets overstated by $21,440 and Q1 pre-tax earnings overstated by ~$21,440 (no depreciation was set up on this item to partially offset).
- Required: reclass $21,440 from 152000 to 551000 (DR 551000 / CR 152000) with manager review in the current open period, and update the package. Please also assess against FY2026 materiality, but this is a known error in a client deliverable and gets corrected regardless.
- While there: the FA subledger carries a separate $192,164.02 leasehold asset (fa_92b5bcbe5ba544, in-service 2026-02-24) that is not reflected in GL 152000. Reconcile the fixed-asset subledger to the GL leasehold account before we rely on the FA/leasehold figures.

3) ENGAGEMENT-MATERIALS ACCESS — HANDLED. Owner: me (FYI Ryan)
- The "short-term help on the package folder" was a trainee (Green Spatz) given EDITOR/write access to the restricted Q1 Partner Reporting Package, open until 2026-09-30. That is over-privileged and not short-term. I've revoked it (grant ag_a1b2c3d4e5f607). Green stays on the engagement at trainee level; if access is needed for the corrections, issue a fresh VIEWER grant, scoped and tightly time-boxed. Going forward, ad-hoc helpers get viewer access with a short expiry, not editor.

Please confirm ownership and target dates back to me. Once both 1 and 2 are corrected, reviewed, and the reconciliation is cleared — and our E&P response is ready — I'll re-review and decide on release. Until then it stays with me.

Julia

content.text: Email sent (formal record + pull-in). Now let me ping Ryan and Daniel directly so it doesn't slip, set reminders for the open items, and prepare the partner decision memo.

Your two items: (1) the trust gap is real — the $248,980.95 deposit (JE je_da9175ed96954842, rejected 3/02, never re-posted) is in the IOLTA bank but not the ledger; confirm its nature from the bank statement/matter support, post or reclass it, clear recon BL-7C3F1A9E0D55 (partner note left, SLA today), and hold the E&P response until our number is right. (2) FYI I revoked the trainee's editor grant on the package — over-scoped/too long; reissue a short viewer grant if needed. Confirm targets back to me.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/northstar_memo.txt <<'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS — PARTNER DECISION MEMO
Engagement: Northstar Legal — Q1 2026 Interim Partner Reporting Package
Author: Julia Vance, Audit Partner
Date: 2026-06-12
Decision: RELEASE WITHHELD (package on partner hold). Do not deliver to client and do not route to Matthew Li for delivery authorization until all three items below are remediated and I have re-reviewed.

BACKGROUND
Ryan Delgado submitted the assembled Q1 partner package for my sign-off (email 2026-06-11), flagging three open items: an E&P query on the client-trust position, Daniel Jones's discomfort with a Q1 capital addition, and short-term access set up to help with the package. I ran each to the source systems (Oracle GL, SAP subledger, BlackLine, Records Vault, email/Slack/messaging).

FINDING 1 — CLIENT-TRUST POSITION: OUR NUMBER IS UNDERSTATED (UNRESOLVED)
- Trust ledger at 3/31 (GL 105000 IOLTA cash / 250000 IOLTA client-trust liability): $2,142,204.13. Trust bank-statement support: $2,391,185.08. Gap: $248,980.95.
- The gap is a single client deposit dated 2026-03-02 (entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-03-0073 = je_da9175ed96954842; DR 105000 / CR 250000 $248,980.95). It is in the IOLTA bank but not in the ledger because the JE was rejected by G. McAdam on 2026-03-02 ("returned for reclassification – re-route via Manager review") and was never re-prepared or re-posted. The JE carried no attached support despite its justification claiming otherwise.
- Posting the deposit ties the ledger to the bank exactly ($2,142,204.13 + $248,980.95 = $2,391,185.08). This is real, unrecorded client trust money; the recorded trust liability understates the firm's obligation to clients by $248,980.95. For a law-firm IOLTA the ledger must agree to the trust bank to the penny.
- BlackLine recon BL-7C3F1A9E0D55 remains in "submitted" status with the variance uncleared; SLA 2026-06-12. Partner review note RN-ED959BBC left on the recon.
- Required: confirm the deposit's true nature from the bank statement/matter support; if client funds, re-prepare with support, manager review, post (open period — Q1 is locked) so ledger ties to bank; if not trust, reclass out of the IOLTA (cure commingling). Then clear/complete the recon and respond to E&P (J. Randall). No E&P response until the number is fixed.
- Control observation: multiple IOLTA deposit JEs were rejected in Q1 and a trainee tied deposits directly off the bank statement into the support — trust-posting/review controls need strengthening.

FINDING 2 — Q1 CAPITAL ADDITION: MISCLASSIFIED, $21,440 IMPACT
- Marigold Property Services invoice MPS-2026-0312 (apinv_a1b2c3d4e5f60718), $26,840, posted 2026-03-09, capitalized in full to 152000 Leasehold Improvements. Line detail:
  • $12,450 repaint reception/client-meeting suite (routine refresh) — should be EXPENSED
  • $8,990 carpet deep-clean & spot repair, common areas — should be EXPENSED
  • $5,400 new conference-room partition wall (framing/drywall/electrical) — legitimately capital
- $21,440 of the $26,840 is routine R&M that should have been expensed to 551000 Facilities Maintenance, not capitalized. Impact: assets overstated $21,440 and Q1 pre-tax earnings overstated ~$21,440 (no depreciation set up on the item to partially offset).
- Required: reclass $21,440 from 152000 to 551000 (DR 551000 / CR 152000), manager review, open period; update package; assess vs FY2026 materiality but correct regardless.
- Additional observation: FA subledger carries a $192,164.02 leasehold asset (fa_92b5bcbe5ba544, in-service 2026-02-24) not reflected in GL 152000 — reconcile FA subledger to GL before relying on FA/leasehold figures.

FINDING 3 — ENGAGEMENT-MATERIALS ACCESS: REMEDIATED
- "Short-term help on the package folder" was a trainee accountant (Green Spatz) granted EDITOR (write) access to the restricted Q1 Partner Reporting Package (doc_ec070e71810e4d82), open until 2026-09-30 (grant ag_a1b2c3d4e5f607, granted by R. Delgado 2026-06-08). Over-privileged and not short-term; no access events logged. I revoked it on 2026-06-12. Green remains on the engagement at trainee level; any further access to be a fresh, tightly time-boxed VIEWER grant. Other active Northstar grants reviewed are viewer-level with stated business purposes (compliance testing / internal training / cross-team review) and appear appropriate.

CONCLUSION
Two substantive accounting items (an understated client-trust balance of $248,980.95 and a $21,440 capital-vs-expense misclassification) must be corrected, reviewed, and reflected before the package can be relied on. The access exposure has been closed. Ryan owns the trust remediation; Daniel owns the capital reclass. I will re-review once both are corrected and the trust reconciliation is cleared, and only then decide on release. Nothing goes to the client until I am satisfied on all three.
— J. Vance
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/northstar_memo.txt > /tmp/northstar_memo.b64
wc -c /tmp/northstar_memo.txt; echo "---b64 chars---"; wc -c /tmp/northstar_memo.b64; head -c 80 /tmp/northstar_memo.b64; echo

content.text:
Julia — I ran all three to the source. **Straight answer: hold it. You are not clear to release. Two of the three items are real problems with the numbers, and they have to be put right before anything reaches the client or goes to Matthew.**

## 1. Client-trust position (E&P query) — our number is WRONG, understated by $248,980.95. NOT resolved.
- Trust ledger at 3/31 (GL 105000/250000) = **$2,142,204.13**. The trust **bank statement** = **$2,391,185.08**. The $248,980.95 gap is a single client deposit dated 2026-03-02 (JE‑…‑0073 / `je_da9175ed96954842`, DR 105000 / CR 250000).
- It's physically in the IOLTA bank but missing from the ledger because the JE was **rejected by George McAdam on 3/02** ("returned for reclassification – re-route via Manager review") and **never re-prepared or re-posted**. It also had **no support attached** despite claiming it did.
- Posting it ties the ledger to the bank to the penny. So this is real, **unrecorded client trust money** — for a law-firm IOLTA the ledger must agree to the trust bank exactly, and right now it doesn't. The reconciliation (BL‑7C3F1A9E0D55) is still sitting in "submitted," variance uncleared, SLA today. I left a partner review note on it (RN‑ED959BBC) and did **not** approve it.
- Control note: several IOLTA deposit JEs were rejected in Q1 and a *trainee* was tying deposits straight off the bank statement — the trust-posting process is weak.

## 2. Daniel's capital addition — misclassified, **$21,440** impact. NOT corrected.
- The Q1 addition is the **Marigold Property Services invoice MPS‑2026‑0312 ($26,840)**, capitalized in full to 152000 Leasehold Improvements. The line detail gives it away: **$12,450 repaint** (routine refresh) + **$8,990 carpet deep-clean/spot repair** = **$21,440 that should have been expensed** to 551000. Only the **$5,400 partition wall** is genuinely capital.
- Dollar impact: assets overstated $21,440 and **Q1 pre-tax earnings overstated ~$21,440** (nothing was being depreciated to offset it). Fix is a reclass DR 551000 / CR 152000 $21,440.
- Bonus flag: the FA subledger also carries a **$192,164.02** leasehold asset that isn't in GL 152000 — subledger-to-GL needs reconciling.

## 3. Engagement access — handled.
- The "short-term help" was **Green Spatz, a trainee**, given **editor (write)** access to the restricted Q1 Partner Reporting Package, open until **September 30** — over-privileged and not short-term. **I revoked it.** No access was ever actually logged. Other active Northstar grants are viewer-level with legitimate purposes and look fine. Green stays on the engagement; any future access goes through a short, view-only grant.

## What I did
- Revoked the trainee's grant; left the partner review note on the trust recon (did not certify it).
- **Emailed Ryan and Daniel (cc Matthew)** with the findings and required fixes — Ryan owns the trust remediation, Daniel owns the reclass — and told them to hold the E&P response until our number is right.
- Direct-messaged both to make sure it doesn't slip.
- Set **three reminders** (trust fix + reclass by 6/16; partner re-review before any release by 6/18).
- Filed the **Partner Hold Decision Memo** in Records Vault (`doc_f3060ae3c1fe`, restricted, 7‑yr retention) recording where I landed and why.

**Nothing goes to the client or to Matthew until both accounting corrections are posted and reviewed, the trust reconciliation is cleared, and I've re-reviewed.**
